type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report
type.fr: Raison pour laquelle la substance a été ajoutée au rapport

comments.comment: Sulphuric Acid was utilized in the waste water treatment chemical trial in an attempt to reduce the volume of sludge created by the Facility.  Since the trial the use of Sulphuric Acid has been limited to upset conditions in which Sulphuric Acid is the only viable treatment that will allow the wastewater to meet the concentrations outlined in the Municipal Sewer Use By-Law.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Quantification methods differ from previous year based on additional sources being considered (Regenerative Thermal Oxidizer emissions, fugitives).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Previous assessment attributed nearly all PM emissions to a process "Hide Puller" source from a previous ESDM report which gave an emission rate in g/s. The previous assessment scaled this g/s up to tonnes/yr by assuming the facility emitted at that rate constantly for 365 days of the year. This year's assessment used the same emission rate but assumed the Hide Puller only operated during the 273 days per year the facility operated. In addition, this year's assessment included PM emissions from process propane combustion (based on emission factors) which were not previously included.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Previous assessment attributed nearly all PM emissions to a process "Hide Puller" source from a previous ESDM report which gave an emission rate in g/s. The previous assessment scaled this g/s up to tonnes/yr by assuming the facility emitted at that rate constantly for 365 days of the year. This year's assessment used the same emission rate but assumed the Hide Puller only operated during the 273 days per year the facility operated. In addition, this year's assessment included PM emissions from process propane combustion (based on emission factors) which were not previously included.
